content.problemStatement:
Chef is very adventurous, so he asked Bob to give him a task.

Bob gave him a sequence of blocks with heights A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. Chef is at the first block and he has to reach the NNN-th block using the minimum number of moves to complete the task.

In one move, he can jump from the iii-th block to the jjj-th block only if the following conditions are satisfied:


i<ji \lt ji<j
Ai≥AjA_i \geq A_jAi​≥Aj​
for all kkk (i<k<ji \lt k \lt ji<k<j), Ak≤AjA_k \leq A_jAk​≤Aj​
You have to find the minimum number of moves Chef needs to perform to reach the last block, or determine that it is impossible.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer --- the minimum number of moves or −1-1−1 if it is impossible to reach the last block.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1045 \cdot 10^45⋅104

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There is no way to move from the first block (with height 999) to any other block.
Example case 2: The only sequence of 444 moves is 20→17→15→8→720 \rightarrow 17 \rightarrow 15 \rightarrow 8 \rightarrow 720→17→15→8→7. For example, in the first move, all the heights between 202020 and 171717 do not exceed 171717, so all conditions are satisfied.
